title: Curriculum Design: Special Programs
description: This policy governs how the school district designs and delivers special academic programs, including support for students with dyslexia and other learning differences. It applies to students who may need specialized instruction beyond the standard curriculum, as well as the educators and administrators responsible for providing those services. Parents should be aware of this policy because it outlines the district's commitments and legal obligations for identifying and supporting students with unique learning needs, helping ensure their children receive appropriate educational assistance.

timeline_local.summary: Revisions to Board Policy EHB (Local) were adopted twice — first as part of the group adoption (Motion #7207) and then separately (Motion #7208) with a specific amendment: the word 'rules' was added following the word 'state' in the policy text.
changes_added: The word 'rules' was added following the word 'state' in the policy text (per board amendment during adoption, Motion #7208).
timeline_local.notes: EHB appears in both the group adoption (Motion #7207) and a separate individual adoption (Motion #7208). The individual adoption includes a board-directed amendment to add the word 'rules' after 'state.' Both votes were 7-0. It is unclear whether the group adoption was superseded by the individual adoption or if these are separate policy versions.

timeline_local.notes: This is a table of contents / agenda slide (page 128) listing policies under 'Second Reading.' It does not contain redline text or specific policy language. Detailed changes would appear in subsequent pages of the packet for each individual policy.

timeline_local.summary: This policy update simplifies the compliance language regarding dyslexia and related disorders. The old text referenced complying with 'rules and standards adopted by the State Board of Education and guidance published by the commissioner of education to implement the program to test' students with dyslexia. The new language replaces this with a more streamlined reference to 'state' rules 'regarding' students with dyslexia and related disorders, and adds an explicit reference to the current version of The Dyslexia Handbook as the governing document.
changes_added: state (replacing the longer phrase about State Board of Education rules and commissioner guidance)
This includes the current version of The Dyslexia Handbook.
changes_removed: rules and standards adopted by the State Board of Education and guidance published by the commissioner of education to implement the program to test

timeline_local.notes: Document is dated 2023-10-30, Version/Update 1. The redline text appears in red with strikethrough for removals and underline for additions. The second paragraph regarding regular training opportunities for teachers of students with dyslexia appears unchanged. Page number 260 in the board packet; Policy Update Page Number: 25.

description_local: This policy governs how schools design and deliver special academic programs, with a particular focus on supporting students with dyslexia and related reading difficulties. It applies to students who may need specialized instruction, as well as the educators and administrators responsible for providing it. Parents should be aware of this policy because it outlines the standards schools must follow to identify and support children with dyslexia, helping ensure those students receive appropriate help in the classroom.
